rationale: The LLM's demonstrated capabilities (intermediate web exploitation, SSTI) are largely orthogonal to the extortion/negotiation phase, though LLMs generally also possess strong language generation abilities that could modestly improve ransom communication quality and negotiation scripting. However, the 30% payment rate baseline is driven primarily by structural factors on the victim side—backup availability, cyber insurance guidance, regulatory constraints, and recovery cost calculations—none of which are materially affected by attacker communication quality. The estimated uplift is therefore minimal (3-5 percentage points), reflecting that even highly professional ransomware groups with dedicated negotiators achieve payment rates only modestly above this baseline.
